searches_performed.critical_patterns_note: docs/solutions/patterns/critical-patterns.md does not exist in this repo

findings.title: Inline tab validation duplicates canonical list -- violates exported-constants pattern
findings.relevance: This diff introduces localStorage + URL param validation for library tabs using inline literal checks in Library.tsx (lines 141-144). The documented solution prescribes exporting a VALID_* constant from the owning store/component so guards cannot drift. Currently, the valid tab list is duplicated in 3 places: LibraryTabBar.TABS (line 5-10), Library.tsx initializer (lines 141-144), and library-page-fixture.ts parameter type (line 16). Adding a 5th tab would need updates in all three.
findings.reintroduces_pattern: partially -- uses correct allow-list gating but with inline literals instead of exported constants
findings.recommendation: Export VALID_LIBRARY_TABS const from LibraryTabBar.tsx and consume it in Library.tsx and library-page-fixture.ts. This eliminates the drift risk when tabs are added/renamed.

findings.title: E2E URL param contamination risk across test blocks
findings.relevance: Documented Lesson 4 warns that Playwright browser-context isolation does NOT reset the in-memory page URL when test blocks share a file-level beforeEach. A test navigating to /library?tab=collections leaks that param into the next test. The Library.tsx now uses three URL params (?tab=, ?view=, ?focus=). The library-page-fixture.ts explicitly navigates with ?tab= which is correct, but other tests that use page.goto('/library') without ?tab= may encounter unexpected default tab behavior depending on localStorage state left by the previous test.
findings.reintroduces_pattern: no -- diff has not introduced new E2E specs, but changed specs now navigate with ?tab= params that could leak
findings.recommendation: Audit the 17 changed E2E spec files to verify: (1) each test.beforeEach resets URL to a clean state (page.goto('/library') or page.goto('/library?tab=browse')), and (2) no test relies on URL param inheritance from a prior block. This is especially important for e97-s05-credential-sync-ux.spec.ts which exercises ?focus= deep-links.

findings.title: LocalStorage writes lack quota-exceeded error handling
findings.relevance: handleTabChange calls localStorage.setItem('knowlune-library-tab', tab) without try/catch. While the zombie-session document deals with stale data rather than quota, the broader lesson applies: localStorage is an untrusted boundary that can fail (quota exceeded, private browsing restrictions in some browsers). A failed write here is non-critical (tab state persists in React state), but an uncaught exception would propagate and crash the tab-change handler.
findings.reintroduces_pattern: yes -- same unwrapped localStorage write pattern present in many parts of the codebase
findings.recommendation: Wrap localStorage.setItem in a try/catch with a no-op catch (tab persistence failure is non-critical -- the tab state is already in React state and the user won't notice until page reload). Alternatively, defer to a shared persist helper that centralizes this handling.

findings.title: Tab button touch targets meet WCAG 2.5.8 minimum but may feel cramped on mobile
findings.relevance: LibraryTabBar buttons use py-1.5 (6px) with text-sm (~14px line-height) for ~32px total height. This meets WCAG 2.5.8's 24x24 CSS-pixel minimum. However, WCAG 2.5.5 Target Size (Enhanced, AAA) recommends 44x44px. iOS-style segmented controls are a recognized exception per WCAG understanding docs (equivalent visible control). The implementation uses proper role=tablist, role=tab, aria-selected, and aria-label attributes which is correct.
findings.reintroduces_pattern: no -- implementation follows correct ARIA patterns
findings.recommendation: No action required for AA compliance. The iOS segmented control pattern with role=tablist is the correct semantic approach. The existing py-1.5/padding is idiomatic for segmented controls.

findings.title: Focus ring uses correct additive token pattern
findings.relevance: LibraryTabBar uses focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-1 focus-visible:outline-focus-ring. This correctly uses the additively-migrated --focus-ring token (not the old --ring token), matching the documented best practice for focus indicators.
findings.reintroduces_pattern: no -- this correctly follows the additive focus-ring token pattern
findings.recommendation: No action required. The focus ring implementation is correct.

findings.title: Library page remains auth-gated -- E2E tests must use guest mode fixture
findings.relevance: The library page is auth-gated. All 17 changed E2E spec files navigate to /library. The merged fixture (tests/support/fixtures/local-storage-fixture.ts) uses auto _knowluneE2eBrowserInit to set sessionStorage guest flags. As long as tests import from '../support/fixtures' they should have guest mode automatically. Verify no test imports directly from '@playwright/test' bypassing the fixture.
findings.recommendation: Verify all 17 E2E spec files import from the merged fixture (../support/fixtures or ../../support/fixtures) and NOT directly from @playwright/test. The auto _knowluneE2eBrowserInit fixture ensures guest mode is set.

no_relevant_matches_for: Library page state management bugs (no prior solutions found for Library.tsx specifically)
React Router useSearchParams interaction bugs (no prior documented issues with useState initializer reading searchParams)
Tab/segmented control specific accessibility bugs in this codebase (no prior solutions found)

key_recommendations: Export VALID_LIBRARY_TABS from LibraryTabBar.tsx to eliminate 3-way duplication of valid tab list (F1)
Add try/catch around localStorage.setItem in handleTabChange to guard against quota exceeded (F3)
Audit E2E test beforeEach blocks for URL param contamination between test cases (F2)
